description: Inkwell spam-scoring corpus. Each case fixes a payload + form + context shape and asserts the score falls in a range. Range-based assertions are tolerant to weight tuning; the corpus catches regressions when a signal flips polarity (e.g. an operator change inverts the all-caps ratio direction). Add a row before changing signal weights.

payload.name: Alice
payload.message: Hi — I wanted to follow up on our chat last week. Could we schedule a call?

cases.name: clean short body just over min length

payload.name: Eve
payload.message: Hallo — wir interessieren uns für Ihre Dienstleistungen.

cases.name: clean technical question with one URL

payload.name: Frank
payload.message: I'm getting a 500 on https://example.com/api/widgets — repro is in the linked notion.

payload.name: Grace
payload.message: Thanks for the demo earlier this week. I had a few questions about how the rule engine handles segment cycles — does the depth limit kick in before the visitor sees a 500, or do you short-circuit at evaluation time?

cases.name: honeypot filled with whitespace only — still cleared by trim

payload.name: Linker
payload.message: Visit https://a.com https://b.com https://c.com https://d.com for deals.

payload.name: Loud
payload.message: BUY NOW LIMITED TIME OFFER ACT FAST OR MISS OUT

cases.name: URLs + disposable + bad email syntax + short
